focus:
Focus on systems that implement semantic operators — LLM-powered relational/dataframe operations such as sem_filter, sem_map, sem_join, sem_topk, sem_agg, sem_search — and on the query engines, optimizers, and execution layers built around them. Emphasis is on system design, cost models, plan optimization, and engineering trade-offs of declarative LLM-based data processing, not on the underlying LLM models themselves.

Included Content (In Scope)

Semantic operator algebras and declarative LLM data-processing languages (LOTUS, Palimpzest, DocETL, ThalamusDB, Aryn, ELEET, SUQL, Eva, BlendSQL, QUEST, etc.)
LLM-powered relational/dataframe primitives: sem_filter, sem_map, sem_join, sem_topk, sem_agg, sem_search, sem_extract, sem_cluster, sem_sim
Query optimization, cost models, and plan rewriting for LLM/semantic operators (cascades, approximate filtering, sampling, model cascades)
Execution engines for semantic operators: batching, caching, speculative execution, plan adaptivity
Semantic indexing, semantic caching, embedding-aware optimization for LLM operator pipelines
Accuracy/quality guarantees, statistical correctness, and probabilistic plans for semantic operators
LLM-as-judge / LLM-as-operator within structured data workflows (tables, documents, knowledge bases)
Integration of LLM operators with SQL, dataframes (pandas, Polars), or dataflow systems
Benchmarks, datasets, and evaluation frameworks specifically for semantic operator engines
NL2SQL / NL2Plan only when it produces or composes semantic operators (not pure text-to-SQL)

Excluded Content (Out of Scope)

Pure LLM prompting/agent papers without a query-engine or operator-level systems contribution
General RAG papers focused on prompting, generation quality, or chunking strategies
Text-to-SQL papers that target only traditional relational SQL (no semantic/LLM operators)
LLM training, fine-tuning, or reasoning research without a data-system contribution
Vector database / ANN index papers (covered by [[VECTOR_DB]])
LLM memory / context-extension papers (covered by [[LLM_MEMORY]])
Application papers that consume off-the-shelf LLM APIs without operator-level system design
